risk_rationale: The draft bill introduces new requirements for age verification and parental consent that are not fully aligned with TikTok's current baseline, posing a material compliance gap that requires immediate attention.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states it uses machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users and remove/limit access.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Requires age verification using commercially available methods.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: TikTok's current age assurance posture relies on machine learning and safety reviews, which may not meet the new requirement for commercially available age verification methods.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Default privacy for teens; accounts for ages 13-17 are private by default.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Mandates parental consent for minors accessing certain online services.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: TikTok's current policy does not require parental consent for minors aged 13-17, which is now mandated by the draft bill.

remediations.title: Implement Commercial Age Verification
remediations.description: Integrate a commercially available age verification system to comply with the new regulatory requirement.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification system is integrated and operational.
System meets regulatory standards for commercially available methods.

remediations.title: Parental Consent Mechanism
remediations.description: Develop a mechanism to obtain and verify parental consent for minors accessing the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Parental consent is required and verified for all minors accessing the platform.
Consent mechanism is compliant with regulatory standards.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new requirements could result in significant legal and financial penalties, as well as potential reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Initiate a project to integrate commercial age verification methods.
Develop and implement a parental consent mechanism.
Engage with legal and regulatory teams to monitor the bill's progress and adjust compliance strategies accordingly.
